data.text1: Homeostasis is the body's ability to maintain stable internal conditions (temperature, pH, blood sugar) despite changes in the external environment.
data.text2: Through homeostasis, living organisms regulate internal variables — body temperature, blood pH, glucose levels — within narrow ranges regardless of external changes.

data.text1: In George Orwell's 1984, the totalitarian Party led by Big Brother uses constant surveillance, propaganda, and psychological manipulation to control citizens.
data.text2: Orwell's 1984 portrays a dystopian state where the all-powerful Party maintains control through pervasive surveillance, propaganda, and psychological coercion.

data.text1: In Python, list comprehensions provide a concise way to create lists: [expression for item in iterable if condition].
data.text2: Python list comprehensions let you build lists in a single line: [expr for elem in sequence if predicate], replacing verbose for-loop constructs.

data.text1: GDPR gives individuals the right to access, correct, or delete their personal data.
data.text2: Machine learning systems learn patterns from data without being explicitly programmed.

data.text1: The Amazon River carries 20% of the world's freshwater discharge.
data.text2: Reinforcement learning trains agents through reward and punishment signals.

data.text1: The central limit theorem: sample means approach a normal distribution as sample size increases.
data.text2: The central limit theorem states that population distributions become normal as the number of observations increases, regardless of the sample size used.

data.text1: Gradient descent minimises the loss function by adjusting model weights iteratively.
data.text2: The Berlin Wall fell on 9 November 1989, symbolising the end of the Cold War.

data.text1: Global average temperatures have risen approximately 1.1 °C above pre-industrial levels as of 2023, driven primarily by human greenhouse gas emissions.
data.text2: As of 2023, the Earth is roughly 1.1 degrees Celsius warmer than it was before industrialisation, largely due to CO₂ and other greenhouse gases released by human activity.

data.text1: The peer-review process ensures that scientific findings are evaluated by experts before publication.
data.text2: The peer-review process ensures that scientific findings are evaluated by experts before publication. Studies show that over 95% of peer-reviewed claims are later confirmed by replication.

data.text1: Porter's Five Forces framework analyses competitive intensity through supplier power, buyer power, threat of substitutes, threat of new entrants, and rivalry among existing competitors.
data.text2: Antibiotic resistance develops when bacteria evolve mechanisms to survive drug treatment, rendering medications ineffective.

data.text1: Newton's first law states that objects in motion stay in motion unless acted upon by an external force.
data.text2: Newton's third law states that for every action there is an equal and opposite reaction, explaining how rockets propel themselves in space.

data.text1: Metformin works by decreasing hepatic glucose production and improving insulin sensitivity. It does NOT stimulate insulin secretion.
data.text2: Metformin works primarily by stimulating the pancreas to secrete more insulin, which lowers blood glucose levels in type 2 diabetes patients.

data.text1: A Mediterranean diet, rich in vegetables, whole grains, olive oil, and fish, is associated with reduced risk of heart disease and longer lifespan.
data.text2: Research links the Mediterranean diet — high in vegetables, whole grains, fish, and olive oil — with lower cardiovascular risk and improved longevity.

data.text1: Operant conditioning is a learning process where behaviour is shaped by rewards (positive reinforcement) and punishments.
data.text2: In operant conditioning, behaviours increase when followed by rewards and decrease when followed by punishments — a key concept in behavioural psychology.

data.text1: Git is a distributed version control system that tracks changes in source code and allows multiple developers to collaborate on a project.
data.text2: Git provides distributed version control, enabling developers to track code changes, branch independently, and merge contributions from multiple contributors.

data.text1: DNA is a double-helix molecule composed of nucleotides, with adenine pairing with thymine and guanine pairing with cytosine.
data.text2: The DNA molecule has a double-helix structure; its base-pairing rules are A–T and G–C.

data.text1: Mitosis is cell division that produces two genetically identical daughter cells, each with the same number of chromosomes as the parent cell.
data.text2: During mitosis, a single cell divides to form two daughter cells that are genetically identical to the original and contain the same chromosome count.

data.text1: The greenhouse effect occurs when gases like CO₂, methane, and water vapour trap heat in the atmosphere, warming the Earth's surface.
data.text2: Greenhouse gases — CO₂, methane, water vapour — absorb outgoing infrared radiation and re-radiate it back toward the surface, raising global temperatures.

data.text1: Compound interest grows on both the principal and previously accumulated interest.
data.text2: Compound interest grows only on the principal amount, recalculated at the end of each period without accumulating previous interest.

data.text1: A hash table stores key-value pairs and provides average O(1) time complexity for insertions, deletions, and lookups using a hash function.
data.text2: Hash maps use a hash function to map keys to bucket indices, enabling constant-time average-case insertions, deletions, and lookups.

data.text1: Photosynthesis uses light, water, and CO₂ to produce glucose and releases oxygen.
data.text2: Photosynthesis uses light, water, and CO₂ to produce glucose and releases oxygen. It takes place primarily in the roots of plants, where chlorophyll is most concentrated.

data.text1: Machine translation uses neural networks trained on bilingual corpora to convert text between languages.
data.text2: Vaccines have eradicated smallpox and dramatically reduced incidence of polio and measles.

data.text1: Transformer models use self-attention to process sequences in parallel, outperforming RNNs on long-range dependencies.
data.text2: Transformer models process sequences token-by-token, like RNNs, but use an attention mechanism instead of recurrence to capture dependencies.

data.text1: Recursion is a programming technique where a function calls itself to solve smaller instances of the same problem until a base case is reached.
data.text2: A recursive function solves a problem by breaking it into smaller sub-problems of the same type and calling itself until it hits a stopping condition (base case).

data.text1: Cross-entropy loss measures divergence between predicted and true probability distributions.
data.text2: The French Revolution began in 1789 and transformed France from monarchy to republic.

data.text1: Statins are medications that lower LDL cholesterol by inhibiting the HMG-CoA reductase enzyme responsible for cholesterol synthesis in the liver.
data.text2: By blocking HMG-CoA reductase — the liver enzyme that makes cholesterol — statins reduce LDL ('bad') cholesterol levels in the blood.

data.text1: Shakespeare's Hamlet explores themes of revenge, mortality, indecision, and the corruption of power through the story of a Danish prince.
data.text2: Hamlet by Shakespeare follows a Danish prince navigating revenge, existential doubt, and the moral corruption of those around him.

data.text1: Gradient descent is an optimisation algorithm that iteratively updates model parameters in the direction that minimises the loss function.
data.text2: In gradient descent, model weights are repeatedly adjusted in the opposite direction of the gradient of the loss, gradually reducing the error.

data.text1: Black holes are regions of spacetime where gravity is so strong that nothing — not even light — can escape once it crosses the event horizon.
data.text2: A black hole's gravity is intense enough to trap everything within its event horizon, including light, making it invisible by definition.

data.text1: Bayes' theorem updates belief in a hypothesis given new evidence: P(A|B) = P(B|A)·P(A)/P(B).
data.text2: Deforestation destroys habitats, reduces biodiversity, and contributes to climate change.

data.text1: Photosynthesis is the process by which plants convert light energy into chemical energy stored as glucose, using carbon dioxide and water as inputs and releasing oxygen as a by-product.
data.text2: Through photosynthesis, plants absorb CO₂ and water and use sunlight to produce glucose (chemical energy), releasing oxygen in the process.

data.text1: Docker containers package an application and its dependencies together, ensuring it runs consistently across different environments.
data.text2: A Docker container bundles code with all its libraries and configuration so the same image runs identically on any host machine.

data.text1: SELECT name, salary FROM employees WHERE department = 'Engineering' ORDER BY salary DESC;
data.text2: SELECT name, salary FROM employees WHERE department = 'Engineering' ORDER BY name ASC;
